text.title: Copy Templates by Search Intent
text.summary: Headline and description patterns for transactional, commercial, and informational search intent.

example.code:
Headlines: - Buy [Product] Online - [Product] Starting at $X - Free Shipping Over $X - Order Today, Ships Tomorrow - Official [Brand] Store
Descriptions: Shop our full [product] collection. Free shipping on orders over $X. Easy 30-day returns. Order by 2pm and get it by [day].
Over 50,000 happy customers. Browse our top-rated [products], backed by a 30-day money-back guarantee. Order securely online.

Headlines: - Best [Product] for [Use Case] - #1 Rated [Product] Platform - Compare [Product] Options - Why 10K+ Teams Choose Us - See All Features Free
Descriptions: Looking for the best [product]? See why [X] teams chose us. Compare features, pricing, and reviews side by side. Start free trial today.
[Year]'s top-rated [product] solution. Trusted by [notable customers]. Full feature access free for 14 days — no credit card required.

Headlines: - Free [Topic] Guide - How to [Achieve Outcome] - [Topic] Explained Simply - Learn [Topic] in 5 Minutes - Free [Resource Type]
Descriptions: New to [topic]? Our free guide covers the essentials: [key point 1], [key point 2], and [key point 3]. Download instantly, no signup.
Master [topic] with our free playbook — used by 25,000+ professionals. Plus, try our [product] free for 14 days to put it into practice.

rule.statement: Create separate ad groups for each intent type with intent-matched headlines — do not mix transactional and informational copy in the same ad group.
rule.rationale: Search intent signals in the query determine what messaging converts. Mixing intents in one RSA degrades Quality Score because some headline combinations will be irrelevant to the query's actual intent.
